role: 2nd President of the Islamic Republic of Iran (August 1981)
bio: Mohammad-Ali Rajai was Iran's second president, serving for less than one month after his election on 24 July 1981. A committed revolutionary and former teacher, he had previously served as prime minister. He and his prime minister [Mohammad-Javad Bahonar](/people/mohammad-javad-bahonar) were assassinated in a bombing at the prime minister's office on 30 August 1981, an attack attributed to the [Mojahedin-e Khalq](/orgs/mojahedin-e-khalq) (MEK).
editorial_notes: Assassinated 30 August 1981. Shortest-serving president in IRI history.
